data.title: Alibaba's Qwen-Scope Open Source Initiative Unveiled
data.description: Alibaba has announced the open-sourcing of Qwen-Scope, an interpretability module derived from the Qwen3 and Qwen3.5 series models. According to Foresight News, the module is designed for various applications, including directional control of inference results, data classification and synthesis, model training and optimization, and evaluation of sample distribution analysis and comparison. The open-source release includes weights from seven large models, encompassing dense models and mixture of experts models from the Qwen3 and Qwen3.5 series, with a total of 14 sets of sparse autoencoder weights.

data.title: Binance Alpha to Remove Multiple Tokens from Featuring List
data.description:
According to the announcement from Binance, several tokens will be removed from the Binance Alpha featuring list due to non-compliance with the platform's standards. The removal is scheduled for 2026-04-30 at 09:30 (UTC). The affected tokens include REX (Revox), XO (Xociety Token), TANSSI (TANSSI), DARKSTAR (DarkStar), YALA (Yala), RCADE (RCADE), RDAC (Redacted), SKATE (Skate), OVL (Overlay Protocol), SLAY (SatLayer), Ghibli (Ghiblification), Ghibli (GhibliCZ), PHY (DePHY Network), VLR (Velora), SVSA (SavannaSurvival), WBAI (Whitebridge Network), EDGEN (LayerEdge), FAIR3 (Fair and Free), MM (MOMOFUN), BUBB (Bubb), AICell (AICell), XLAB (Dexlab), and SIGHT (Empire of Sight).

Binance emphasizes its commitment to fostering innovation and transparency while prioritizing user protection. Despite the removal, users will still be able to sell or withdraw these tokens on Binance Alpha. To do so, users can navigate to the Binance Wallet's Market tab to search and trade, or use the Asset tab in Binance Alpha to select the token for instant selling or withdrawal. Binance advises users to exercise caution, as tokens on Binance Alpha may pose higher risks and be subject to significant price volatility. Users are encouraged to conduct thorough research to understand the projects before trading.

Binance also highlights the importance of risk management and advises users to be aware of potential discrepancies between the original English content and any translated versions. The platform has introduced a regular review mechanism to remove unqualified tokens, enhancing transparency and alerting the community to fraudulent third-party impersonations. Binance reserves the right to amend or cancel this announcement at any time without prior notice. Users are reminded that digital asset prices are subject to high market risk and volatility, and they should consult independent financial advisers before making investment decisions.

data.title: XO Market Surpasses $150 Million in Trading Volume Since Beta Launch
data.description: XO Market is challenging competitors Polymarket and Kalshi with its user-generated prediction market model. According to NS3.AI, the platform has recorded over $150 million in trading volume since its mainnet beta launch last November. XO Market reports having more than 30,000 users and over 600 active markets.

data.title: AhnLab Blockchain Secures South Korean VASP License for Digital Asset Services
data.description: AhnLab Blockchain Company has received a Virtual Asset Service Provider (VASP) license in South Korea, enabling it to offer digital asset transfer, custody, and management services. According to NS3.AI, the Financial Intelligence Unit of South Korea's Financial Services Commission granted this approval on April 23. AhnLab Blockchain Company intends to leverage this license to establish a Web3 financial infrastructure business aimed at corporate clients.

data.title: YF Financial Group Invests in WooshPay for Strategic Expansion
data.description:
YF Financial Group has completed a strategic investment in WooshPay, a global financial infrastructure and cross-border payment service provider. According to ChainCatcher, the specific investment amount has not been disclosed.

WooshPay operates under strict regulations from the UK's Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) and Hong Kong Customs. The platform offers cross-border fund transfer services to prominent clients such as Xiaomi, large educational groups, and IoT-listed companies. Its operations span 170 countries and regions, supporting 155 fiat currencies and major digital assets.

YF Financial aims to integrate WooshPay's advanced global payment settlement capabilities with its digital asset infrastructure, AlphaToken. This integration seeks to establish a comprehensive ecosystem that includes asset tokenization and compliant settlement, creating a full-chain ecological loop.

data.title: Hungary Seeks More Rights for Hungarians in Ukraine Before EU Talks
data.description: Hungary has expressed its desire for increased rights for ethnic Hungarians residing in Ukraine as a condition for supporting Ukraine's formal negotiations to join the European Union. Bloomberg posted on X that Hungary's stance highlights the ongoing tensions between the two nations over minority rights. The Hungarian government has been vocal about its concerns regarding the treatment of ethnic Hungarians in Ukraine, particularly in the context of language and education rights. This issue has been a point of contention in Hungary-Ukraine relations, affecting broader diplomatic engagements. Hungary's position could potentially impact Ukraine's EU accession process, as unanimous approval from all EU member states is required for the initiation of formal negotiations. The situation underscores the complex interplay of regional politics and minority rights in Eastern Europe.

data.title: Energy Transition Accelerated by Iran Conflict, UNFCCC Reports
data.description:
The ongoing conflict in Iran is significantly accelerating the global shift towards renewable energy, according to a report by the United Nations Framework Convention on Climate Change (UNFCCC). Bloomberg posted on X that the geopolitical tensions are prompting countries to reconsider their energy strategies, focusing more on sustainable sources.

The UNFCCC highlights that the instability in the region is driving nations to reduce reliance on fossil fuels, which are subject to volatile market conditions. This shift is seen as a necessary step to ensure energy security and mitigate the impacts of climate change.

Experts suggest that the conflict is acting as a catalyst for governments to invest in clean energy technologies, aiming to diversify their energy portfolios. The report emphasizes the urgency of transitioning to renewable energy to avoid future disruptions in energy supply.

The UNFCCC's findings underscore the importance of international cooperation in achieving a sustainable energy future. The organization calls for increased investment in renewable infrastructure and innovation to meet global energy demands sustainably.

As the situation in Iran continues to evolve, the global energy landscape is expected to undergo significant changes, with renewable energy playing a crucial role in shaping future policies.

data.title: SHIB Whale Sells 800 Billion Tokens for $4.9 Million, Profits Exceed $660 Million
data.description: An original gangster (OG) whale has reportedly sold 800 billion Shiba Inu (SHIB) tokens today for $4.9 million, according to NS3.AI. This transaction has brought the whale's total profit to over $660 million. Initially, the wallet invested $13,760 to acquire 103.33 trillion SHIB tokens. Despite the recent sale, the wallet still retains 99.27 trillion SHIB, valued at approximately $625.41 million.

data.title: Eurozone Inflation Rises in April, Driven by Energy Costs
data.description: Eurostat reported on April 30 that inflation in the Eurozone surged in April due to soaring energy costs, providing the European Central Bank (ECB) with a rationale for interest rate hikes. However, moderate underlying price growth data reduced the urgency for immediate action. According to Jin10, the preliminary annual CPI rate for April jumped from 2.6% in the previous month to 3.0%, exceeding the ECB's 2% target, with energy costs accounting for most of the increase. Meanwhile, core inflation, excluding volatile food and energy prices, slowed down. Inflation in the services sector, a persistent component of the price basket, decreased from 3.2% to 3.0%, while non-energy industrial goods inflation, which has been a major drag on prices, rose to 0.8%. These mixed data present a challenge for the ECB, which is likely to keep interest rates unchanged at its meeting tonight, although it may signal an increasing likelihood of policy tightening. The high overall inflation data supports the case for rate hikes, but the underlying data suggests that the initial energy shock has not yet led to significant secondary effects.

data.title: A whale withdrew 9,600 ETH from Bybit within 5 hours, and currently holds a total of 17,800 ETH.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a whale withdrew 9,600 ETH from Bybit in the past 5 hours, worth $21.6 million. This address currently holds a total of 17,811 ETH, worth $40.2 million.

data.title: Examining the Cycles of the Crypto Industry Through a Century of Technological History
data.description:
The crypto industry has experienced various cycles of growth and decline, reflecting patterns seen throughout a century of technological advancements. According to PANews, the industry has undergone significant transformations, influenced by technological innovations and market dynamics. These cycles are reminiscent of historical trends observed in other tech sectors, where periods of rapid development are often followed by phases of consolidation or decline.

The article highlights how the crypto industry, much like previous technological revolutions, has faced challenges such as regulatory scrutiny, market volatility, and evolving consumer expectations. Despite these hurdles, the industry continues to adapt and evolve, driven by advancements in blockchain technology and increasing interest from institutional investors.

The analysis draws parallels between the crypto sector and past technological shifts, suggesting that understanding these patterns can provide insights into the future trajectory of digital currencies and blockchain applications. As the industry matures, stakeholders are keenly observing how these cycles will influence long-term growth and stability.

Overall, the examination of the crypto industry's lifecycle through the lens of technological history offers a perspective on its potential for resilience and innovation amidst changing market conditions.

data.title: Public Sale Participants Face Restrictions as Prices Plummet
data.description: Public sale participants are reportedly unable to claim their tokens, watching helplessly as prices drop to zero without the ability to sell. According to PANews, there are suspicions that insiders or market makers have been selling large quantities at high prices.

data.title: Eurozone April CPI Annual Rate Estimated at 3%
data.description: The Eurozone's consumer price index (CPI) for April was estimated at an annual rate of 3%, surpassing expectations of 2.9%, according to Jin10. This preliminary figure also marks an increase from the previous rate of 2.6%.

data.title: KAST Appoints Former SEC Advisor as Head of Corporate and Policy Communications
data.description:
Stablecoin payment company KAST has appointed former U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) senior advisor Stephanie Allen as its new head of corporate and policy communications. According to Foresight News, this appointment aims to accelerate KAST's business growth in North America and Latin America.

During her tenure at the SEC, Allen provided internal and external communication strategies to KAST's leadership and served as a spokesperson. She also advised the SEC's cryptocurrency task force. KAST was founded by former Circle executive Raagulan Pathy in July 2024 and offers USD-denominated accounts, global payments across 170 countries, and a suite of stablecoin-based financial tools for consumers and businesses.

data.title: Japan Exchange Group Considers Launching Cryptocurrency ETFs Next Year
data.description: Japan Exchange Group (JPX) is contemplating the introduction of exchange-traded funds (ETFs) that track cryptocurrencies as early as next year, provided legal amendments are made. According to Foresight News, JPX CEO Hiromi Yamaji stated that asset management firms have shown significant interest in developing cryptocurrency ETF products. He emphasized that JPX is ready to proceed once legislative and tax conditions are clarified. However, JPX also noted that if progress is delayed, the launch might be postponed until 2028.

data.title: XO Market Secures $6 Million Seed Funding to Enhance Prediction Platform
data.description: XO Market has successfully closed a $6 million seed funding round aimed at expanding its user-generated prediction market platform. According to NS3.AI, the platform allows users to create markets and set fees, fostering a dynamic trading environment. Since the commencement of its mainnet beta in mid-November, XO Market has reported over $150 million in trading volume and facilitated more than 600 user-created markets. The company plans to introduce XO Vaults in the coming weeks, enabling users to pool capital into market-making strategies.

data.title: Eurozone Economic Data Release Imminent
data.description: The Eurozone is set to release key economic indicators shortly. According to Jin10, the March unemployment rate, the preliminary annual GDP growth rate for the first quarter, and the initial annual and monthly CPI rates for April will be announced in ten minutes.

data.title: Wasabi Protocol Faces Suspicious Fund Movements on Ethereum and Base Chains
data.description:
Wasabi Protocol is experiencing unusual fund movements across its contracts on Ethereum and Base chains, according to Foresight News. BlockSec monitoring indicates potential misuse of administrator privileges, involving approximately $5.15 million.

Initial signs suggest that an account funded by Tornado Cash was later granted ADMIN_ROLE permissions, participating in processes related to WasabiLongPool, WasabiShortPool, and WasabiVault.

data.title: White Tech Gains MiCA Authorization in Croatia
data.description: White Tech has been granted authorization by Croatia's HANFA to function as a crypto-asset service provider under the European Union's Markets in Crypto-Assets (MiCA) regulation. According to NS3.AI, this approval enables White Tech to provide exchange, transfer, custody, and administration services within the EU's unified crypto framework. White Tech is among the pioneering companies in Croatia to receive MiCA authorization, marking a significant step in the region's crypto regulatory landscape.

data.title: Alberta Investment Management Corp Acquires MicroStrategy Shares
data.description: Alberta Investment Management Corp, a major Canadian institutional asset manager, has disclosed its initial purchase of 1.38 million shares of MicroStrategy (MSTR), valued at $219 million. According to Odaily, this marks the institution's first allocation into Bitcoin-related assets.
